To implement the checkout and reset commands in the existing minigit            
implementation, we need to:                                                     

 1 Checkout Command: This command should allow users to switch between different
   commits or branches. For simplicity, we'll assume that each commit is a      
   directory within .minigit/commits.                                           
 2 Reset Command: This command should allow users to reset their working        
   directory and staging area to match the state of a specific commit.          

Here's how we can extend the main.ml file to include these commands:            

main.ml                                                                         

                                                                                
@@ -1,4 +1,5 @@                                                                 
                                                                                
             printf "Staged: %s\n" file                                         
           else                                                                 
             printf "Tracked: %s\n" file)                                       
                                                                                
+let checkout commit_id =                                                       
+  let dir = ".minigit/commits/" ^ commit_id in                                 
+  if not (Sys.file_exists dir) then                                            
+    failwith "Commit does not exist"                                           
+  else                                                                         
+    Sys.copy_dir dir "." ~exclude:[".minigit"]                                 
+    |> ignore;                                                                 
+    printf "Checked out to commit %s\n" commit_id                              
+                                                                               
+let reset commit_id =                                                          
+  let dir = ".minigit/commits/" ^ commit_id in                                 
+  if not (Sys.file_exists dir) then                                            
+    failwith "Commit does not exist"                                           
+  else                                                                         
+    Sys.copy_dir dir "/tmp/minigit_reset" ~exclude:[".minigit"]                
+    |> ignore;                                                                 
+    Sys.remove_dir_rec ".minigit/staged";                                      
+    Unix.mkdir ~perm:0o755 ".minigit/staged";                                  
+    Sys.copy_dir "/tmp/minigit_reset" "." ~exclude:[".minigit"]                
+    |> ignore;                                                                 
+    printf "Reset to commit %s\n" commit_id                                    
+                                                                               
 let () =                                                                       
   match Sys.argv with                                                          
   | [| _; "init" |] -> init ()                                                 
   | [| _; "add"; file |] -> add file                                           
   | [| _; "commit"; message |] -> commit message                               
   | [| _; "status" |] -> status ()                                             
+  | [| _; "checkout"; commit_id |] -> checkout commit_id                       
+  | [| _; "reset"; commit_id |] -> reset commit_id                             
   | _ -> eprintf "Usage: minigit <command>\n"                                  
                                                                                

This implementation includes the checkout and reset commands. The checkout      
command copies the files from a specific commit to the working directory, while 
the reset command resets the staging area and working directory to match the    
state of a specific commit.                                                     

To verify that this implementation passes all tests, you can run:               

                                                                                
bash test-v2.sh                                                                 
                                                                                

This should ensure that your minigit implementation is functioning correctly    
with the new commands.
